title: D² messaging — messages spec
description: Schema for contracts/mq-messages/mq-messages.spec.json — the source-of-truth catalog of every message type D² publishes. DcsvIo.D2.Mq.SourceGen reads this to emit the MqMessages static class (string constants) + the runtime registry (constant → MqMessageDescriptor) into DcsvIo.D2.Messaging.Abstractions. Each entry pairs one message type with its publisher contract: target exchange, exchange type, encryption domain, default routing key. The producing service owns its entries.

constant.description: PascalCase identifier emitted as a const string under MqMessages. Must be unique across all entries (D2MQ003).

messageType.description: Fully-qualified .NET type name of the message class. Runtime resolver verifies the type carries [MqPub] referencing this entry's constant.

exchange.description: AMQP exchange name. Convention: d2.{producer}.{purpose} (lowercase, hyphen-separated within segments). Validated by the regex above.

exchangeType.description: AMQP exchange type. fanout = broadcast (every binding receives every message); topic = routing-key-pattern matching; direct = exact-routing-key matching. headers + x-* exchange types intentionally excluded — not used by D² conventions.

encryption.description: Either an EncryptionDomains constant value (e.g. 'audit', 'comms', 'files') OR the literal string 'plaintext'. Default-deny: a missing or unknown value fails the build (D2MQ004).

encryptionReason.description: REQUIRED when encryption == 'plaintext'. Free-form rationale shipped into audit logs / code review. Forces the dev to make a conscious decision per plaintext message type.

defaultRoutingKey.description: Routing key used by publishers when no per-publish override is supplied. Empty for fanout. For topic exchanges, may include placeholder tokens like {channel} for runtime substitution (template support deferred — currently treated as literal).

deprecated.description: When true, the entry is deprecated: the generated C# constant + factory carry [Obsolete] and the TS constant + factory carry @deprecated. Additive — the entry is NEVER deleted. Deleting a deprecated (or non-deprecated) entry is a breaking change the JSON-diff gate fails.

deprecatedReason.description: Plain dev-facing English explaining why the code is deprecated; rendered into the [Obsolete("...")] message, the @deprecated JSDoc, and the changelog entry. NOT a TK key (developer-facing, not user-facing).

replacedBy.description: Wire code of the successor entry (e.g. RESOURCE_NOT_FOUND). Must reference a real, non-deprecated entry in the merged registry. Omit for pure retirement with no replacement.

sunset.description: ISO-8601 date for the future RFC 8594 Sunset response header. Edge-runtime-deferred: carried on the contract now, consumed by the Edge response middleware when it exists. Inert until then.
